title: Team Management
lede: Invite your staff as team members, give each person a role and the exact features they should see, and turn access on or off at any time.

steps.title: Open Team Management
steps.content:
1. In the left navigation, open the Account group and click Team (or go to /user-management).
  2. The Users tab lists everyone on your account. The account owner is shown with an Owner badge; everyone you add is a sub-account (team member) under you.
  3. The tabs across the top are Users, Groups (Permission Groups), and View Profiles. Start on Users.

Only the account owner and members you give the User Management permission can manage the team.

images.caption: The Users tab lists the owner and every team member with their role and access toggles
The Users tab lists the owner and every team member with their role and access toggles

steps.title: Add a team member
steps.content:
1. On the Users tab, click Create (Add User).
  2. Enter the member's First Name, Last Name, Email, and a starting Password.
  3. Pick a Role from the list (see the next step). You can leave it blank and set it later.
  4. Click Save. The member can now sign in with that email and password.

For safety, Billing and Credentials access are always turned off for a brand-new member. Turn them on later only if you trust the person with payments and API keys.

steps.title: Give the member a role
steps.content:
• A Role decides which data a member can read, add, edit, or delete. Roles are managed in the Roles and Permissions areas of this page.
  • Start from a ready-made role template (for example Manager, Agent, or Viewer) — selecting it creates a real, editable copy for your account.
  • Open a role's permissions to fine-tune each area: allow Read, Create, Update, or Delete, and set the scope (own records only, or the whole account).

Assign the same role to everyone who does the same job so their access stays consistent.

steps.title: Control visibility with Groups and View Profiles
steps.content:
• Permission Groups (Groups tab) decide whose records a member can see. A member with account-wide scope only sees records owned by people in their group — great for splitting a large team into pods.
  • View Profiles (View Profiles tab) are reusable presets of feature toggles and pricing. Assign a profile to a member to apply the same page-visibility rules in one click.
  • On each user row you can quickly set the member's Groups and View Profile without opening a modal.

steps.title: Turn features on or off per member
steps.content:
1. On a member's row, use the toggles and settings buttons to control access to Billing, Credentials, Automation, CRM, Call Center, Campaigns, API Keys, and User Management.
  2. Open Call Center settings to hide individual sub-pages (Phone numbers, Call Logs, Live Calls, and more) from that member.
  3. Use the per-member Billing option to set a custom per-minute price or hide prices from that member.

steps.title: Activate, deactivate, or assist a member
steps.content:
• Use Enable / Disable on a member's row to instantly allow or block their sign-in — no need to delete the account.
  • As the owner, use Impersonate to view the app exactly as that member sees it, so you can help them or check their access.
  • Update a member's name or role at any time from the same row.

support.content:
